verdict.note: 7/7: claude-shaped payloads, agent_id prefix order, block semantics, launch branching, real deliverViaRendezvous parse-confirmed
init grows .opencode plugin+skills+exclude; narrowing harnesses prunes and sweeps .opencode itself

Reproduced: second send to an opencode worker under normal board-probe pressure exits 1 ('kicked by concurrent connects 3× — prompt NOT delivered') yet the prompt LANDED — opencode's db shows 2 duplicate user injections from the one send (deliver's own retries), pane shows 3 SECOND-OK answer turns. Root cause: the generated plugin daemon awaits injectPrompt INSIDE the line-parse loop, so repaint-done is never written before a probe connect kicks the connection — the parse-confirmed contract (harness.ts replyViaSocket) is broken on the daemon side.

note: mis-anchored: scenario declaration was uncommitted at filing; re-filing after the declaration commit

verdict.note: Reproduced: second send to an opencode worker under normal board-probe pressure exits 1 ('kicked by concurrent connects 3× — prompt NOT delivered') yet the prompt LANDED — opencode's db shows 2 duplicate user injections from the one send (deliver's own retries), pane shows 3 SECOND-OK answer turns. Root cause: the generated plugin daemon awaits injectPrompt INSIDE the line-parse loop, so repaint-done is never written before a probe connect kicks the connection — the parse-confirmed contract (harness.ts replyViaSocket) is broken on the daemon side.
pi-dispatched worker cross-validated generated opencode plugin footprint, event/rendezvous bridge, launch tail branching, and 8/8 mechanical tests

Fixed: same scenario, same probe pressure (0.3s board snapshots), fresh opencode worker with the regenerated plugin — send exits 0 ('sent'), opencode's message store shows exactly ONE injected user message, pane shows one prompt + one SECOND-OK answer. Daemon now parse-confirms synchronously (repaint-done at parse time, injection behind the confirm); unit regression drives the real deliver under a kicking connect storm with a turn-length prompt call — fails on the old generator, passes now (9/9, tsc clean). A/B pair with the 06:24Z fail reading.
Re-measured after the daemon parse-confirm fix: full suite 9/9 (now includes the probe-pressure regression — kicking connects + turn-length prompt call → parse-confirmed ok, ONE injection).

Re-measured after the generator change: real spex init --harness claude,codex,opencode in a scratch repo materializes .opencode/plugins/spexcode.ts (carrying the FIXED sync parse-confirm daemon, dispatch.sh baked), skills dir, AGENTS.md managed block, all exclude-hidden; narrowing harnesses to claude,codex + spex materialize prunes every .opencode artifact and sweeps the emptied home.

Live A-side: gate rejection DOES re-enter the conversation and the loop closes (active→awaiting→asking, agent's own informed ask) — but the injected text is the raw {"decision":"block"} wire JSON with escaped \n, not the extracted reason; plugin reason()=err||out and stop-gate blocks on stdout. Same family as pi 2dfd78e7.

Live: worker's own `spex session ask --note '<真问题>'` from the worktree → record asking, note verbatim on board (ls + timeline), CLI confirmation + 50-char truncation notice shown to agent; asking frees its concurrency slot.
Re-measured in the live matrix: idle send to asking opencode worker under board-probe pressure → exit 0 'sent', opencode.db shows exactly ONE user injection of the token. Holds since the 06:33Z fix.

Live: send during an in-flight turn (12s bash sleep loop visibly running) → exit 0 parse-confirmed 'sent', exactly ONE injection in opencode.db, message honored in the next turn (COUNT.md tail). No duplicate, no false negative.

Live: dirty tree + done --propose merge → Stop gate rejects naming the uncommitted file + commit-first ritual; second undeclared stop gets the terse one-liner (taught-sentinel downgrade); worker lands asking, never a false review. Edge path review→working→asking.

B-side after the reason() fix: fresh worker from a worktree backend running the fixed generator — full teaching text injected as readable prose (real newlines, bullet menu, no wire JSON), commit-gate rejection equally readable, agent flowed active→done→(gate)→close-pending. A/B pair with the 10:11Z fail reading.

Live: temp surface:hook node (PreToolUse block:true) + materialize in the worker worktree → Write FORBIDDEN.md aborted in-process (file never created), reason quoted verbatim by the agent; node removed + re-materialize → same write passes. Manifest-driven, no opencode restart.

Live: kill -9 the agent pid → board liveness offline (pid dead + no socket listener; authored asking untouched); resume → online with fresh agent.pid AND a daemon provably answering on the socket. No immortal working.

A-side: --session resume reattaches the SAME conversation (25.6K context, old tail visible) BUT the resumed worker is unsteerable — send → reply-rejected, prompt NOT delivered: plugin rootSession only adopts from bus events and a resumed session fires none until poked. Continuity ✓ steerability ✗.

B-side after the steerability fix: --session route seeds rootSession from the launch env (send lands with ZERO bus events, token recalled verbatim); --continue route's session.list fallback adopts the true conversation (cleared harness_session_id re-captured to the SAME id). Both routes continuous AND steerable. A/B pair complete.

Live re-measure on both matrix workers (one closed via the live backend, one via a sibling worktree backend): 0 tmux / 0 procs / 0 worktrees / 0 branches / 0 sockets / clean git-worktree registry; store dir retired with the session as documented.

A-side: whole lifecycle measured live via the matrix — all clauses pass EXCEPT the Session trailer: a real worker commit (06d8b002) is unstamped. prepare-commit-msg only knows CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID/CODEX_THREAD_ID; opencode's identity tier (uncontaminated SPEXCODE_SESSION_ID, the envSessionId rule) is missing from the hook.

B-side: hook's new SPEXCODE_SESSION_ID tier stamps a real opencode worker commit (7ab71a4c → Session: d835cefd…). All e2e clauses now measured passing live: lifecycle, capture, stop-gate reinject, mid-run send, dual-route resume, dual-signal liveness, commit attribution. A/B pair complete.

post shim-runtime refactor retest: real dispatched opencode worker (OpenCode 1.18.3) answered and settled undeclared; the gate's parsed REASON re-entered via client.session.prompt (readable, real newlines/bullets, zero '"decision"' wire JSON in pane), agent declared ask --note, record flowed working→asking; harness_session_id captured (ses_0901e…).

post shim-runtime refactor retest: second-message send to the live worker under normal board-probe pressure — exit 0 'sent', pane injection count exactly 1 (PAPAYA answered); a follow-up steer send also landed exactly once (GUAVA). Multi-connection runtime server: probes cannot kick a delivery, parse-confirm before the injection turn.
